evidence: Job 1910599 on nvl72d022-T13 reached the runtime-bootstrap announcement, then task 0 exited 1 before any curl or uv output; elapsed time was 00:02:23.
hypothesis: The container runtime did not receive a valid writable temporary directory.
reproduction: Submit the functional wrapper on aws_dfw with the login image's stale TMPDIR environment.
root_cause: Diagnostic job 1911208 showed that bash -lc restored a stale login-image TMPDIR after the /runtime mount was established.
symptom: Functional job failed silently at runtime_bootstrap before curl.
tested_change: Instrument the host/Pyxis/login-shell boundary and verify the /runtime mount independently of TMPDIR.
verification_evidence: Job 1911208 isolated the post-login environment overwrite; the production fix still requires a new remote verification job.

evidence: Job 1911208 on nvl72d042-T04 found /runtime/tmp mounted from local NVMe, root:root mode 755 and writable as uid 0, while bash -lc observed TMPDIR=/lustre/fsw/portfolios/nemotron/projects/nemotron_sw_post/users/sna/tmp; equality with /runtime/tmp failed and touching the stale path returned ENOENT.
hypothesis: Pyxis propagation alone could override the image's stale temporary-directory value.
reproduction: Start the aws_dfw Pyxis image with host TMPDIR=/runtime/tmp and inspect TMPDIR inside bash -lc.
root_cause: Login-image initialization overwrote TMPDIR after Pyxis environment setup; the mount and permissions were not the failure.
symptom: A valid /runtime/tmp mount existed, but the login shell selected a nonexistent Lustre TMPDIR.
tested_change: Keep host TMPDIR at the host runtime path, stop propagating TMPDIR through Pyxis, and export /runtime/tmp as the first command inside every container shell.
verification_evidence: Job 1911208 proved the boundary diagnosis; host regression tests simulate the stale post-login value, while remote verification remains pending.

evidence: Job 2362239 passed runtime_bootstrap and Bridge validation (203 passed, 2 deselected, 82 warnings), then slurm.out lines 649-650 reported `Preserved: -c: line 74: syntax error: unexpected end of file` and task 0 exit 2.
hypothesis: Raw single-quoted printf formats closed the outer single-quoted bash -lc payload and fragmented its argv.
reproduction: Capture the exact focused-tests srun invocation from commit 9c32c95e7 with a stand-in SRUN executable and inspect its argv and stdin.
root_cause: The focused-tests phase nested raw single-quoted printf strings inside an outer single-quoted bash -lc command. Host parsing removed quotes, split the payload into 17 arguments, changed Bridge printf %s\n into %sn, and left the inner script syntactically incomplete.
symptom: Focused validation failed after Bridge passed, before the NeMo unit tests and remaining gates completed.
tested_change: Convert only the focused-tests srun to bash -s with a quoted BASH heredoc; execute-capture the exact invocation, parse its complete stdin with bash -n, and verify two literal Bridge diagnostic lines.
verification_evidence: The focused executable regression passes locally and all preserved gate contracts remain covered; remote verification is pending.

evidence: Job 2362298 passed runtime bootstrap, Bridge (203 passed, 2 deselected), NeMo (5 passed), unit-result preservation, and Pyrefly (0 errors). The first Ruff hook passed; the second Ruff hook modified the renderer, reported one fixed import-format error, and the task exited 1.
hypothesis: The committed renderer retained one extra blank line that Ruff 0.9.9's import-format gate would remove.
reproduction: Run Ruff 0.9.9 with `check --select I` against the committed renderer from 219f4eb56, or run the parent's changed-file pre-commit gate.
root_cause: The renderer committed at 219f4eb56 was not normalized by the parent's exact Ruff import gate: two blank lines separated `from typing import Any` from `REQUIRED_PHASES`, while Ruff required one.
symptom: Remote functional validation passed the repaired quoted-heredoc boundary, then failed in parent pre-commit before GPU smoke and GRPO.
tested_change: Delete only the extra blank line and verify the original Ruff 0.9.9 I001 reproduction changes from RED to GREEN; retain the existing pre-commit configuration unchanged.
verification_evidence: Remote quoted-heredoc verification succeeded through Bridge, NeMo, unit-result preservation, and Pyrefly. Local Ruff regression passes after the one-line normalization; the full remote functional gate remains pending because job 2362298 did not reach GPU smoke or GRPO.

evidence: Job 2349175 passed focused validation, both Ruff hooks, Cutlass import, and Transformer Engine forward/backward on four GB200 GPUs. GRPO completed setup and rollout generation, then a rank-local one-sample logprob shard failed microbatching with configured size 2.
hypothesis: The 1n4g child inherited a 4n4g logprob microbatch size that exceeded its rank-local rollout shard.
reproduction: Resolve the 1n4g recipe at 1fa29cf21: rollout batch 2 prompts x 2 generations equals 4, DP is 4 from world/(PP x CP x TP), and inherited logprob_batch_size 2 does not divide the one-sample rank-local shard.
root_cause: The 1n4g child recipe inherited `policy.logprob_batch_size=2` from the 4n4g parent. With `data_plane.enabled=false`, plain `Policy._shard_for_logprob` pre-sharded the four-sample rollout batch over DP=4 into one sample per rank before MegatronPolicyWorker used the configured logprob microbatch size, triggering the exact 1 modulo 2 assertion.
symptom: The functional gate passed four-GPU GB200 smoke and generated rollouts, then failed in get_logprobs before the first training update.
tested_change: Set only the 1n4g child recipe's logprob_batch_size to 1 and require the rollout-derived rank-local shard to be divisible by that value; ON and OFF continue to share the same recipe and workload.
verification_evidence: The resolved-recipe regression changes from 1 % 2 failure to a one-sample rank-local logprob microbatch. Remote verification is pending; job 2349175 supplies GPU-smoke evidence but no completed training update or performance result.

evidence: Job 2362710 passed focused validation and four-GPU GB200 smoke, loaded logprob_batch_size 1, completed rollout, and entered fused expert logprob forward. All four policy ranks then failed in Transformer Engine padding with `GetTransformerEngineDType: Invalid type (7)`.
hypothesis: The pinned Transformer Engine padding extension does not support the FP64 router-probability tensor produced by the inherited recipe setting.
reproduction: Resolve the 1n4g recipe at 806f55da to inherited moe_router_dtype fp64, then trace MCore router Double probabilities through quantized TEGroupedMLP `_fused_forward` into Fp8Padding and the pinned TE 2.15 dtype converter, which has no Double case.
root_cause: The 1n4g child inherited `moe_router_dtype=fp64`. MCore therefore produced Double router probabilities (PyTorch ScalarType 7), and its quantized op-fuser path padded them before fused expert execution. Pinned Transformer Engine 42b84005 does not map Double in `GetTransformerEngineDType`, so padding failed on every policy rank.
symptom: The functional gate passed four-GPU smoke and the prior logprob-batch boundary, then failed during fused expert logprob forward before the first training update.
tested_change: Set only the shared 1n4g recipe's moe_router_dtype to fp32, matching Qwen/MoE and FP8 performance recipes. Both ON and OFF retain identical router precision, op-fuser configuration, topology, and workload; only the CuTeDSL selector differs.
verification_evidence: The resolved-recipe dtype contract changes from inherited fp64 to explicit fp32. Job 2362710 proves the earlier logprob_batch_size=1 fix crossed its boundary, but remote dtype verification remains pending and no training update or performance result exists.

evidence: Job 2362916 at source 30693c629 passed focused validation and four-GPU GB200 smoke, resolved moe_router_dtype fp32, crossed the prior Invalid type (7) boundary, and completed one update. At step 2, policy and generation profiling began before vLLM CPU backup and the four policy ranks' mature optimizer-state offload; Slurm recorded step 2362916.4 OUT_OF_MEMORY, exit 0:125, MaxRSS 633145024K, and four oom_kill events.
hypothesis: Nsight trace collection beginning exactly at step 2 reduced host-memory headroom while vLLM retained 32.32 GiB per rank on CPU and all four policy ranks concurrently copied newly materialized optimizer state to CPU.
reproduction: Run the three-update functional gate at 30693c629 with NRL_NSYS_PROFILE_STEP_RANGE=2:3; after the first optimizer update, observe profiling start, vLLM sleep, four approximately 105.7 GiB policy allocations entering offload, and the Slurm cgroup OOM.
root_cause: The immediate failure was a host-memory cgroup OOM during the synchronized step-2 refit/offload, not a CUDA OOM. Mature Adam state, vLLM's CPU-backed sleep allocation, and active Nsight collection overlapped; the available evidence does not apportion their exact byte contributions.
symptom: The first training update completed, then all four policy workers died during the next refit's optimizer offload.
tested_change: Profile update 1 with range 1:2, retain three functional updates so profiling stops before the mature step-2 optimizer offload, and use two updates for profile-only ON/OFF arms. Timing arms remain unprofiled.
verification_evidence: Job 2362916 proves the FP32 router fix and first update, but its first-step metrics are warm-up and non-authoritative. No ON/OFF speedup or performance conclusion is supported. Local wrapper and report verification covers the scheduling and evidence change; remote verification is pending.

evidence: Job 2363067 proved the 1:2 profile window stopped both profilers before step-2 refit, but all four policy workers still died while concurrently offloading mature Adam state; Slurm classified step 2363067.4 as OUT_OF_MEMORY with four oom_kill events.
hypothesis: vLLM level-1 sleep keeps a CPU weight backup that is redundant for full-weight refit and consumes approximately 32.32 GiB per generation worker, leaving insufficient host-cgroup headroom for four concurrent mature optimizer-state copies.
reproduction: Run the three-update functional gate at 17addcb6a with profile range 1:2. Observe profiler stop at the start of step 2, then four approximately 105.7 GiB policy allocations enter optimizer offload and the Slurm step receives four OOM kills.
root_cause: The repeated failure is a host-memory cgroup OOM during mature optimizer-state offload even after profiling has stopped. The second run disproves active profiling as a necessary cause; vLLM's level-1 CPU weight backup remains a large avoidable resident allocation for this full-weight-refit workflow.
symptom: Step 1 and its Nsight capture completed, then the four policy actors died during step-2 refit before the second update.
tested_change: Keep intermediate dynamic-sampling and validation sleeps at level 1. Use the validated discard_weights=True interface only after a rollout batch is complete and the next generation is guaranteed to perform a full refit; that refit-safe boundary maps to vLLM sleep level 2.
verification_evidence: Job 2363067 validates profile stop ordering and disproves the prior sufficient-cause hypothesis. Its step-1 values are warm-up/non-authoritative and support no speedup claim. Local lifecycle and recipe contracts pass; remote discard/refit verification is pending.

evidence: Running the documented aggregate refresh with no completed result directories replaced eight bounded manual incidents with an empty JSON array; the committed report contract failed on missing job 1910599.
hypothesis: Aggregate refresh assumed every incident was regenerated from a completed run directory, but this experiment also keeps manually curated bounded evidence under report/evidence.
reproduction: Run the documented --refresh-experiment-dir command while results and benchmark_results contain no completed run directories, then inspect report/incidents.json.
root_cause: refresh_aggregate initialized incidents as an empty list and populated it only from discovered run events, so valid report-root manual incidents were discarded.
symptom: A deterministic report refresh erased the existing root-cause timeline.
tested_change: Seed refresh from existing incidents whose run ID and exact evidence/job-<run_id>.txt path pass the renderer's bounded evidence contract; continue rebuilding run-derived incidents from discovered runs.
verification_evidence: The focused regression changed from one failure to one pass, and the full recipe/report suite passes 58 tests after a real aggregate refresh preserved all prior incidents.

evidence: Job 2363339 passed Bridge validation (203 passed, 2 deselected), the focused NeMo validation, all 10 lifecycle regressions, and Pyrefly with 0 errors. The parent Ruff import hook then rewrote tests/unit/algorithms/test_grpo.py and the task exited 1 before GPU smoke or functional GRPO.
hypothesis: The committed unittest.mock import was not in Ruff 0.9.9's canonical import order and would be rewritten by the parent's exact pre-commit gate.
reproduction: At source fe4af31799f2f10bfdd011d5454d339394eb869d, run Ruff 0.9.9 check --select I against tests/unit/algorithms/test_grpo.py and observe it split the aliased call import from MagicMock and patch.
root_cause: The lifecycle regression test combined aliased and unaliased unittest.mock imports on one line. Ruff 0.9.9 normalized them into separate imports; pre-commit treats hook modifications as failure, so the functional gate exited before GPU work.
symptom: Focused validation passed, but parent pre-commit failed before GPU smoke and functional GRPO.
tested_change: Retain Ruff's import-only rewrite and verify the pinned lint, import-sort, and format checks without changing lifecycle behavior.
verification_evidence: Job 2363339 verifies the focused lifecycle tests but provides no GPU-smoke, training-update, timing, throughput, or performance evidence. Ruff 0.9.9 verification passes locally; remote functional verification is job 2363729.

evidence: Job 2350825 passed focused validation, Cutlass import, and four-GPU Transformer Engine forward/backward smoke. It completed one policy update, instantiated GroupedGemmGluSm100, and captured four approximately 30-31 MB policy-worker Nsight reports. During step 2, all four policy ranks entered optimizer offload at approximately 105.70-105.71 GB GPU allocation; Slurm classified step 2350825.4 OUT_OF_MEMORY with exit 0:125 and MaxRSS 453952640K.
hypothesis: Either discard_weights=True did not resolve to vLLM sleep level 2 at runtime, or level 2 completed but the remaining four-rank mature optimizer offload still exceeded the 940000 MB host-memory cgroup. Existing vLLM logs do not distinguish these cases.
reproduction: Run the three-update Lyris functional gate at fb97eb1bb on one four-GPU GB200 node. Observe one completed update, weight/KV wake, vLLM sleep messages, and the synchronized step-2 policy optimizer offload followed by host OOM.
root_cause: The immediate failure was a host-memory cgroup OOM during the four policy ranks' synchronized mature optimizer-state offload. Sleep calls occurred, but the run did not log their requested/resolved level or process/system-memory delta, so the avoidable vLLM contribution remains unresolved rather than proven fixed.
symptom: The GB200 integration run crossed all smoke gates and one optimizer update, then policy actors died during step-2 optimizer offload.
tested_change: Add fail-visible driver and sync/async worker logging for discard_weights, requested/resolved sleep level, process RSS, system available memory, and before/after deltas; rerun without changing the workload so the two memory hypotheses can be separated.
verification_evidence: Job 2350825 verifies CuTeDSL smoke execution and one warm-up update only. Direct lifecycle-memory verification and a completed functional gate are pending; no ON/OFF speedup is supported.

evidence: Read-only job 2351138 reproduced human-table kernel-name truncation and successfully generated a 47,101-byte CSV from a job-2350825 policy profile. The CSV contained 144 BlockScaledMoEGroupedGemmGluBiasKernel instances and 48 BlockScaledMoEGroupedGemmDgluDbiasKernel instances with no ellipsis. The job then exited 127 because its final diagnostic grep was incorrectly shell-quoted.
hypothesis: CSV output would preserve full names, but existing word-boundary regexes might still reject class tokens embedded without delimiters inside generated CUTLASS identifiers.
reproduction: Run default and CSV cuda_gpu_kern_sum reports against one retained job-2350825 .nsys-rep, then test the exact forward/dgrad class-token regexes against the CSV Name field.
root_cause: The functional evidence command used Nsight's human-readable table, which truncated kernel names. CSV preserved the names, but the full class tokens were concatenated with surrounding generated identifier characters, so leading/trailing word boundaries also produced false negatives. Job 2351138's nonzero status was a separate postprocessing quote error after both stats exports completed.
symptom: Real fused kernels existed in the retained profile, but the strict functional validator could classify activation as absent.
tested_change: Emit cuda_gpu_kern_sum as CSV and retain the exact forward/dgrad class tokens while removing only their invalid word-boundary anchors; cover the real generated-identifier shape in the wrapper regression.
verification_evidence: The real CSV has zero ellipses, 144 fused-forward instances, and 48 fused-dgrad instances. Local bash syntax, 52 wrapper tests, Ruff, and diff checks pass. The next functional job must verify the changed evidence path end to end; this diagnostic supports no speedup claim.

evidence: Job 2363981 completed the locked bootstrap, passed 10 focused lifecycle tests including the new driver/sync/async sleep-level and host-memory telemetry assertions, and reported Pyrefly 0 errors. Parent pre-commit then rewrote one third-party import-order line and exited before GPU smoke or GRPO.
hypothesis: The test file used an import order accepted by the local current Ruff but not by the remote pre-commit hook's pinned Ruff version.
reproduction: At source ed4450b68, run the parent's pinned Ruff import hook over tests/unit/models/generation/test_vllm_generation.py and observe pytest/psutil reordering after all focused lifecycle tests pass.
root_cause: The new psutil import was placed before neither all third-party imports nor the ordering required by pinned Ruff 0.9.9. Version-skewed local validation missed the exact pre-commit ordering, and hook mutation is a deliberate failure condition.
symptom: Authoritative lifecycle-memory tests passed, but the functional wrapper stopped in parent pre-commit before GPU work.
tested_change: Apply the remote hook's exact psutil, pytest, ray import order and verify it with both pinned Ruff 0.9.9 and the local current Ruff before resubmission.
verification_evidence: Job 2363981 establishes Linux GREEN for all 10 lifecycle tests and no performance evidence. Commit 6a699c45f passes both Ruff versions locally; job 2364090 is the clean remote verification.

evidence: Job 2364090 passed the full source/test/lint gate, four-GPU GB200 smoke, one warm-up optimizer update, and captured four policy Nsight reports. CSV evidence recorded the exact fused forward and dgrad class names on every rank. Step 2 then entered four-rank mature optimizer offload and Slurm classified step 2364090.4 OUT_OF_MEMORY with MaxRSS 455362368K and two OOM kills.
fix_commit: pending fail-visible lifecycle and policy-offload telemetry
hypothesis: The logger.info-based telemetry was suppressed by Ray worker logging, so the run cannot distinguish whether runtime level 2 released the expected vLLM host allocation or whether the policy optimizer copy alone exceeded the remaining host headroom.
reproduction: Run the three-update functional gate at 6a699c45f on one four-GPU GB200 node. Observe all gates and step 1 succeed, then step-2 synchronized optimizer offload and host OOM; search all retained runtime logs and find lifecycle events only in focused-test output.
root_cause: The immediate training failure remains host-memory cgroup OOM during mature optimizer offload. Separately, the diagnostic failure is now proven: normal module INFO logging is not emitted by these Ray workers, so the newly added level/RSS observations were absent at runtime even though their unit tests passed.
symptom: The complete functional path reached real fused CuTeDSL policy training and one update, but died at step-2 optimizer offload without runtime sleep-memory evidence.
tested_change: Use flushed structured stdout for driver and vLLM worker lifecycle events, and add best-effort policy-worker RSS/system-available snapshots before grad movement, before optimizer movement, and after offload.
verification_evidence: The run proves fused forward/dgrad activation and repeated host OOM, not speedup. The next run must show actual runtime level/RSS lines and cross the step-2 boundary before any timing matrix is submitted.
